name: Tier 1 Materials
pages: This page shows all available $(thing)tier 1 materials$(). $(br)$(li)$(l:smithing/tier_1_materials#royal_steel_ingot)Royal Steel$() $(li)$(l:smithing/tier_1_materials#cursed_gold_ingot)Cursed Gold$()

pages.text: $(item)Royal Steel Ingot$() has metallic and jewellic, high durability and enchantment affinity.
You can find this template in the Village's Weaponsmith. Trade with $(l:smithing/jewelcrafting_table)Jeweler$(/l) villager can also get this template.
This is the first item you should upgrade with Royal Steel, as with it, you no longer need to consume any smithing templates during the forging process.
No matter how you use $(item)Royal Anvil$(), it will not break, and can let you forget "Too Expensive!". Use $(item)Name Tag$() with special codes can also modify item name's formats.

You can remove $(#fb0505)curses$() and repair costs by using $(item)Gold Ingot$() on $(item)Royal Grindstone$(), and normal enchantments will be maintained. The gold ingots will turn into $(l:smithing/tier_1_materials#cursed_gold_ingot)Cursed Gold Ingot$(/l).
Use Royal Steel Ingot to upgrade your tools. This is the first step of their growing path.
Use Royal Steel Ingot to upgrade your tools. This is the first step of their growing path.
Use Royal Steel Ingot to upgrade your tools. This is the first step of their growing path.

An item emanating a curse aura. The carriers will get $(thing)debuffs$(), and hitting by a lightning bolt when using $(item)Royal Anvil$(). $(br2)Gold Ingot will turn into Cursed Gold Ingot when using $(l:smithing/tier_1_materials#royal_grindstone)Royal Grindstone$() to remove curses and repair costs from items. $(br2)$(item)Piglin$() holding curse gold will be zombified and drop additional curse gold upon death.

When you fully using $(item)Cursed Gold Block$() as the base of beacons and use $(item)Cursed Gold Ingot$() to activate it, the beacon will have chance to convert into $(l:smithing/corrupted_beacon)Corrupted Beacon$(/l).
